title: Logic Apps Workflow Validation — Schema Checks, Common Errors, Pre-Deployment

confirmed_facts.fact: Workflow definition schema URL: https://schema.management.azure.com/providers/Microsoft.Logic/schemas/2016-06-01/workflowdefinition.json# — must be present in definition.$schema
ARM template schema URL: https://schema.management.azure.com/schemas/2019-04-01/deploymentTemplate.json# — for the outer ARM envelope
Validate ARM template without deploying: POST /subscriptions/{subId}/resourcegroups/{rgName}/providers/Microsoft.Resources/deployments/{name}/validate?api-version=2022-09-01
Common error: InvalidTemplate — usually means a malformed expression (unbalanced brackets, typo in function name, wrong argument count)
Common error: ConnectionNotFound — the workflow references a connection in $connections parameter that doesn't exist in the connection objects
Common error: DuplicateActionName — two actions in the definition have the same key name (case-sensitive comparison)
Common error: CircularDependency — action A runAfter B, and B runAfter A (directly or transitively)
Common error: TriggerNotFound — the 'triggers' section is empty or missing. Every workflow needs exactly one trigger.
runAfter references must match exact action names — mismatched case or typo in an action name causes a deployment failure with 'ActionNotFound' error

implementation_notes:
=== EXPRESSION VALIDATION (basic) ===
```python
def check_expressions(definition: dict) -> list[str]:
    warnings = []
    text = json.dumps(definition)
    import re
    # Find all @...() expressions
    exprs = re.findall(r'@([a-zA-Z]+)\(', text)
    known = {'triggerBody','triggerOutputs','body','outputs','actions','items','item',
             'variables','parameters','workflow','trigger','concat','substring','replace',
             'split','join','toLower','toUpper','trim','length','contains','empty',
             'first','last','union','intersection','skip','take','sort','reverse',
             'range','int','float','string','bool','json','xml','array','createArray',
             'base64','base64ToString','uriComponent','utcNow','addDays','addHours',
             'addMinutes','addSeconds','addToTime','subtractFromTime','startOfDay',
             'startOfMonth','startOfHour','dayOfWeek','dayOfMonth','dayOfYear',
             'formatDateTime','convertFromUtc','convertToUtc','ticks','parseDateTime',
             'add','sub','mul','div','mod','min','max','rand','and','or','not','if',
             'equals','less','lessOrEquals','greater','greaterOrEquals','coalesce',
             'setProperty','removeProperty','addProperty','xpath','nthIndexOf','slice',
             'chunk','startsWith','endsWith','indexOf','lastIndexOf','decodeBase64',
             'decodeDataUri','dataUri','dataUriToBinary','guid','encodeUriComponent'}
    for expr in set(exprs):
        if expr not in known:
            warnings.append(f"Unknown expression function: @{expr}() — check spelling")
    return warnings
```
caveats: Local structural validation catches 80% of issues but cannot validate: expression syntax correctness, connector-specific input schemas, or MSI permission availability
ARM validate endpoint requires the resource group to exist — it's a server-side dry-run, not purely offline
Connection references in Consumption use @parameters('$connections')['name']['connectionId'] — the exact path varies slightly between connector types
Action names with special characters (hyphens, dots) work in the portal but can cause issues in expression references — use underscores
The workflow definition schema (2016-06-01) has not been updated for newer features — schema validation alone may not catch all issues
